recipe.epochs_rationale: reduced from the old-mix run's 3 (ep3-overtraining suspicion, DIAGNOSTIC_SYNTHESIS.md finding 2 / exp1 confirmed ep1 0.4381 > ep3 0.372)

training.loss_note: 9.04 -> 3.06 over 256 steps. Terminal loss is FAR above the old-mix run's 1.46 (gen02_run.json) -- expected and healthy: the rebuilt mix is 1028 rows (vs 563) of more heterogeneous content (53.5% real wired code + 46.5% judge CoT), so less memorization in fewer epochs; the old run's 1.46 on 563 homogeneous rows was the overfit signature (recipe_provenance_audit.md 3.2).

in_driver_full_val.disposition: KNOWN MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT, not format collapse: the in-driver gate scores all 141 val rows including the 21 replay (code-gen) rows that legitimately carry no [/REASONING]. 18/141 terse at temp0 and 54/423 (=18x3, identical 0.1277 rate) at temp0.7 are bounded by and deterministic over that 21-row replay set. PROVEN by the canonical cot+ctf gate: 0 terse in 480 cot+ctf samples across both temps -- every terse hit lives in the replay rows. Same artifact as 21-02 (gen02), pre-documented in scripts/tinker_fs_gate.py's docstring.

fs_gate.note: Canonical result is CLEANER than the old-mix run (0/360 vs 3/360 at temp0.7) despite the gen share rising from 13% to 53.5% -- rebalancing toward real code did not erode the judge-format stability of the cot/ctf streams.
